element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundational traversal in open jungle space
Primary melee combat mechanic against small enemies
Ranged attack and puzzle-solving tool; boulders as platforms
Basic enemy encounters with predictable behavior
Rocks can shield Chuck; introduces vertical threat awareness
Consumables and rideable platforms expanding traversal options
More aggressive enemy type; requires kicking mechanic mastery
Crocodile seesaw platforming; environmental hazard management
Movement control changes; verticality challenge on mountain
Culmination of world mechanics; Garry Gritter final boss in Graveyard
Final world; thematic climax for rescue narrative

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating difficulty across five-world structure with environmental hazards increasing in World 3-5; early worlds (Jungle, Cave) establish mechanics with moderate challenge; mid-game (Water, Ice) introduces environmental threats; late-game (Graveyard) combines all mechanics for boss confrontation

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus items scattered in elevated or off-path locations requiring boulder platform construction or animal ally use

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction in Jungle world with basic enemies and simple rock-throwing puzzles; establishes belly thrust and boulder mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Water and Ice worlds introduce environmental hazards (drowning, slipping physics) alongside existing enemy types; requires integrating multiple mechanics
difficulty_curve.late_game: Graveyard world combines all prior mechanics with intensified enemy spawning and boss battles; culminates in final Garry Gritter confrontation
notable_spikes: Water world transition: first environmental hazard type (drowning); crocodile seesaw platforming
Ice world: physics change from horizontal-focused to vertical mountain climbing with slippery surfaces
Each world's boss battle: skill check combining world-specific mechanics

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: World structure (3-5 levels per world + boss)
notable_level_patterns.description: Five themed worlds (Jungle, Cave, Water, Ice, Graveyard) each containing multiple standard levels followed by a boss encounter; allows progressive skill escalation within themed space
Levels require throwing and positioning boulders as platforms to reach bonus items or progress; combines combat resource (rocks) with spatial problem-solving
Each world type introduces a signature hazard (falling rocks/enemies in Jungle; water in Water world; slippery ice in Ice world)
Periodic encounters with consumable flying lizards, rideable brontosaurus, or crocodile seesaws that temporarily expand traversal capability or provide resource advantage
